mnemonics: Many Runners In A Race (MRIAR): Imagine multiple runners (threads) starting from the same starting line (start() method) and racing independently.
EXTENDS or IMPLEMENTS? Remember: You can't extend the Thread class if you already extend another, so you must implement Runnable (Java's single inheritance rule).
keyPoints: You can create multiple threads by instantiating multiple Thread objects, each with the same or different Runnable targets.
Calling start() on each Thread object schedules it for execution; the JVM and OS handle the actual concurrent scheduling.
All threads share the same memory space (heap) but have their own call stacks, allowing them to execute different parts of code simultaneously.
commonMistakes: Calling run() instead of start(), which executes the code in the current thread instead of creating a new one.
Assuming threads will start or execute in a specific, predictable order, which is not guaranteed.
